name: release-notes-translation
prompt:
Write release notes for v2.3 of TaskPilot, our project-management app. Here's what engineering gave me:
- BREAKING: removed legacy /v1/tasks API endpoints
- Fixed race condition in sync worker that could drop edits made on two devices simultaneously
- Added bulk-edit for up to 500 tasks at once
- Migrated export pipeline to streaming (was timing out on workspaces over 100k tasks)
- Upgraded internal logging library
Make them user-friendly.
expected_output: User-facing release notes: breaking change flagged first, each engineering item translated to a user outcome, internal-only change omitted or de-emphasized, no filler.
assertions: Delivers actual release notes, not advice on how to write them
The breaking change (v1 API removal) is flagged prominently at or near the top, not buried
Engineering items are translated into user outcomes (e.g. the sync race condition becomes 'edits no longer lost when editing on two devices'; streaming export becomes 'large exports now complete')
The internal logging upgrade is omitted or clearly de-emphasized rather than presented as a user-facing change
No vague filler like 'improved performance' or 'various bug fixes' without specifics
